customer_anxiety_profile: Shirley is a high-intent, cash-capable buyer who is anxious about her credit score — she doesn't understand why it's low given her financial behavior (cash home, cash car, paid-up cards). She is not price-anxious; she is process-anxious. She wants to understand what she qualifies for and how payment structure works before committing.
unresolved_concerns: Credit score of ~611 — Shirley does not understand why it's low and wants it run; she has not been told whether she qualifies for financing or only purchase options
Payment structure — she asked explicitly whether she can purchase in three payments or must pay all at once; this was not answered
Roof age — unknown; Shirley said 'it's in my package' but did not look it up; closer cannot complete qualification without this
Utility bill / usage data — Shirley has been in the home two months and has no bill history; closer will need to pull usage data directly
sensitive_topics_raised: Credit score self-disclosure (~611) — Shirley is sensitive about this and volunteered extensive financial context to compensate; do not lead with credit as a barrier
Cash-buyer identity — Shirley explicitly frames herself as someone who pays cash and dislikes monthly bills; financing framing will land poorly
exact_promises_rep_made: 'We'll send someone by to give you the cost' — language implies in-person visit; closer should clarify format at the top of the call
closer_should_know: Shirley is a self-identified cash buyer with strong purchase intent — she volunteered 'I have the capital, I have a black card' and asked about payment structure unprompted. The credit-score question was left unresolved on the setter call; she expects the closer to run her credit and explain her options. Come prepared with the purchase path and payment structure details.
